source_pr.title: executor: add CTEExec and CTETableReaderExec
source_pr.body:
Signed-off-by: guo-shaoge <shaoge1994@163.com>

<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #24808 <!-- REMOVE this line if no issue to close -->

Problem Summary: This PR implements **CTEExec** and **CTETableReaderExec**. 

The implementation can be divided into three parts:
1. executor/builder.go: construct CTEExec and CTETableReaderExec from physical plans.
2. executor/cte.go: implements CTEExec. 
3. executor/cte_table_reader.go: implements CTETableReaderExec.

**NOTICE**: Because this PR relies on other PR, so I rebased my commit. Please just review this commit.
`Files changed -> choose changes from commits only make by guo-shaoge`

Proposal: [CTE proposal](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/24147) <!-- REMOVE this line if not applicable -->

How it works:
**executorBuilder**
1. Since multiple CTEs can share a temporary storage, a map is recorded in StmtCtx. CTEs with the same StorageID will use the same cteuilt.Storage.

**CTEExec**
1. Compute seed part and store results into `e.iterInTbl`, which will be read by CTETableReaderExec.
2. Compute recursive part and store results into `e.iterOutTbl`.
3. Swap data in `e.iterInTbl` and `e.iterOutTbl`. So the previous iteration's output will be next iteration's input. And the next iteration starts.

**CTETableReaderExec**
1. Just read data in `e.iterInTbl` and output chunk. 

### Related changes

- PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->


- Integration test: cte_test.go


### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- executor: add CTEExec and CTETableReaderExec

symptoms: "select c1 from cte1 order by c1;") (executor/cte_test.go)
failure_signature: "select c1 from cte1 order by c1;") (executor/cte_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: "select c1 from cte1 order by c1;") (executor/cte_test.go).
fix_pattern: "select c1 from cte1 order by c1;") (executor/cte_test.go)

evidence: evidence: "select c1 from cte1 order by c1;") (executor/cte_test.go)
